title: Rajma

overview.description: A comforting North Indian kidney bean curry gently simmered with onions, tomatoes and warming spices. Simple, economical and one of India's great everyday meals.

ingredients: 4 × 400g tins kidney beans, drained and rinsed
method: Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Cook the onions for 10–12 minutes until soft and beginning to caramelise.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chilli, then cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the kidney beans, water and half of the garam masala.
Simmer gently for 30–40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Lightly mash a small portion of the beans against the side of the pan to naturally thicken the curry.
Season with salt and stir in the remaining garam masala.
Finish with freshly chopped coriander before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Rajma is one of the most popular everyday meals in North India, especially in Punjab, where it's traditionally served with rice as Rajma Chawal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build flavour by cooking onions slowly, balancing spices and naturally thickening a bean curry.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the best value meals in the planner. Nutritious, filling, family-friendly and ideal for keeping portions in the freezer.
chapman-tips: Cooking the onions properly is the secret to a rich curry.
Mash a few beans into the sauce rather than adding flour or cornflour.
Add the garam masala in two stages for a fresher flavour.
Homemade chapatis turn this into a complete feast.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in meal-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Often tastes even better the following day. Serve with fresh rice or use as a filling for wraps.
